Show metadata for one corpus item by ID. Never reads document content. Usage: corpus-show <corpus_id> Example: corpus-show corpus_0011

Propose a new Forge command. Forge generates Python code and saves a proposal. You then review it and install it through the normal patch workflow. No code is written to main.py until YOU run patch-ap
Show the full detail of a command proposal — including the exact Python code that will be installed if you approve it. Usage: forge-command-show-proposal <slug> Tip  : forge-command-list-proposals to
List all command proposals — both pending and approved. Usage: forge-command-list-proposals

Approve a proposal and create a patch proposal file in ~/forge/proposed_patches/. After this, use the normal patch workflow to actually install the command. Usage: forge-command-approve-proposal <slug
List all LLM-generated commands that have been installed via patch-apply. Usage: forge-command-list-active
Show the code template used when generating new commands. Helps you understand what forge-command-propose will produce. Usage: forge-command-template

Show exactly what will change if you install a command proposal. No files are written. You must review this before running forge-command-install. Usage: forge-command-install-dry-run <slug> Shows thre

Install an approved command proposal into main.py. Usage: forge-command-install <slug> What this does step by step — clearly: 1. Loads the approved proposal 2. Shows a dry run summary 3. Asks you to t
Verify that an installed command is present in main.py and tool_registry. Usage: forge-command-install-verify <slug>

Regenerate the function code for an existing proposal using the fixed code generator. Use this to fix proposals that failed to install due to the quote-in-description bug. Usage: forge-command-regen-p
list all Python files in the current scope LLM-implemented via qwen3:8b by forge-command-implement

Ask local Qwen3 to write a real implementation for an installed scaffold command. The generated code is saved for review. Nothing changes in main.py until you run forge-command-implement-install. Usag
Review the LLM-generated implementation before installing it. Usage: forge-command-implement-review <slug>

Replace the TODO scaffold in main.py with the LLM-generated implementation. Requires typing IMPLEMENT to confirm. Backs up first. Auto-restores if compile fails. Usage: forge-command-implement-install
Verify the integrity of the forge_audit.log hash chain. Reports broken chain but does NOT exit the session. If chain appears broken, run audit-repair first.

Scan the audit log for multi-line entries (caused by newlines in reason strings) and collapse them into single lines. Run this if audit reports a broken chain. Usage: audit-repair What this does: 1. R

Generate a real implementation for an installed command using local keyword-based templates. No Ollama required — works instantly. After this, use forge-command-implement-review then forge-command-imp

Search PyPI for packages matching a query. Uses the PyPI search page — shows top results with descriptions. Usage: forge-tool-search <query> Example: forge-tool-search image processing Example: forge-
Get details about a PyPI package: version, description, homepage, license. Usage: forge-tool-info <package-name> Example: forge-tool-info rich

Install a PyPI package into Forge's Python environment. Requires typing INSTALL to confirm. Usage: forge-tool-install <package-name> Example: forge-tool-install rich What this does: 1. Fetches package
Generate a Forge command wrapper for an installed package using Qwen3. Creates a proposal that goes through the normal install pipeline. Usage: forge-tool-wrap <package-name> Example: forge-tool-wrap
List all packages installed via forge-tool-install. Usage: forge-tool-list
List all generated tool wrappers and their install status. Usage: forge-tool-wrap-list

Install a generated tool wrapper as a live Forge command. Requires typing WRAP-INSTALL to confirm. Backs up main.py first. Auto-restores if py_compile fails. Usage: forge-tool-wrap-install <package-na
Forge wrapper for rich 15.0.0 Generated by forge-tool-wrap via qwen3:8b

Read main.py and build a full map of every command Forge knows. Extracts function names, docstrings, patch numbers, write flags. Saves the map to memory for report and gap analysis. Usage: forge-self-
Display the self-inspection report from the last forge-self-scan. Shows every command grouped by patch and category. Usage: forge-self-report
Compare what Forge can do against expected capabilities and show gaps. Identifies categories that are weak or missing entirely. Usage: forge-self-gaps
Ask Qwen3 to suggest new Forge commands based on the gap analysis. Each suggestion is saved as a command proposal ready to install. Usage: forge-self-suggest Run forge-self-gaps first to generate the
